summary.intent: Add unit tests for the MR approval rules feature that was recently implemented in release_from_fbc.py, covering the new _load_mr_approvers_from_group_config method and the approval rules setup in create_shipment_mr.
summary.outcome: Added 17 new unit tests across 3 test classes (TestLoadMrApproversFromGroupConfig, TestCreateShipmentMrApprovalRules, TestSetMrApprovalRules) covering dict validation, dry-run behavior, error handling, and GitLab API interactions. All 35 tests pass.

repo: The project uses unittest framework with MagicMock/AsyncMock for testing async code
Test files follow the pattern test_{module_name}.py with test classes named Test{FunctionName}
Existing tests mock exectools.cmd_gather_async for doozer command calls

code.finding: The dry-run check occurs after get_mr_from_url() is called, which causes API errors with placeholder URLs in dry-run mode
The _load_mr_approvers_from_group_config method validates that YAML parsed output is a dict before returning, returning empty dict for non-dict results
The approval rules setup is wrapped in try/except to prevent MR creation from failing if approval rules configuration fails

workflow: AsyncMock must be used for mocking async methods; regular MagicMock causes 'object Mock is not awaitable' errors
When mocking repository objects with async methods like create_branch, the entire repo object should be an AsyncMock
Tests can be run incrementally with pytest to verify changes before committing

friction: Initial edit attempt was blocked because the assistant was in Ask mode, requiring a mode switch
First test implementation used MagicMock for async methods, causing 'object Mock is not awaitable' runtime errors that required fixing
open_items: PR review suggests moving dry-run check before MR fetch in set_mr_approval_rules (defense-in-depth improvement in gitlab.py:139-154)
Need to commit and push the new tests (user approved at end of transcript but action not shown)
Consider adding docstring note about fallback behavior when all usernames fail to resolve (fbladilo review comment)
